revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 二階堂神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[福島県]][[須賀川市]]宮先町78 |位置 = {{Coord|37|17|22.78|N|140|22|28.06|E|region:JP-07_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=二階堂神社}} |祭神 = [[二階堂氏]] |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = }} '''二階堂神社'''（にかいどうじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]の[[須賀川城]]址に鎮座する[[神社]]。城主の[[二階堂氏]]を祀る。
「日本三大火祭り」と称される毎年11月の「松明あかし」の御神火はこの神社で採火され、須賀川市内を巡り、会場の翠ヶ丘公園へ運ばれる<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 二階堂神社]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html 二階堂神社]</ref>。境内の大ケヤキは須賀川市天然記念物に指定されている。
== 交通 == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]] [[東北本線]] [[須賀川駅]]（車で約6分） *[[東北自動車道]][[須賀川インターチェンジ|須賀川IC]]（車で約10分）
== 脚注 == <references/> == 外部リンク == *[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 二階堂神社] 福島県観光情報サイト *[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html 二階堂神社] 須賀川市観光物産振興協会
== 関連項目 == * [[須賀川城]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Nikaidō Shrine |Image = |Location = [[Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Sukagawa City]] 78 Miyasaki-cho |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|17|22.78|N|140|22|28.06|E|region:JP-07_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nikaidō Shrine}} |Deities = [[Nikaidō clan]] |Rank = |Founded = |Main hall = |Alias = |Special designation = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = }}
'''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[Sukagawa City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. It enshrines the [[Nikaidō clan]], the lords of the castle.
The "[[Taima Akashi]]" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. A large zelkova tree on the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Sukagawa City.
== Access == * [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Sukagawa Station]] (approx. 6 minutes by car) * [[Tōhoku Expressway]] [[Sukagawa Interchange|Sukagawa IC]] (approx. 10 minutes by car)
== Notes == <references/>
== Related Topics == * [[Sukagawa Castle]] * [[Taima Akashi]]
== External Links == * [https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine] Fukushima Prefecture Tourism Information Site * [https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine] Sukagawa City Tourism and Product Promotion Association

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Nikaidō Shrine |Image = |Location = [[Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Sukagawa City]] 78 Miyasaki-cho |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|17|22.78|N|140|22|28.06|E|region:JP-07_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nikaidō Shrine}} |Deities = [[Nikaidō clan]] |Rank = |Founded = |Main hall = |Alias = |Special designation = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = }}
'''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[Sukagawa City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. It enshrines the [[Nikaidō clan]], the lords of the castle.
The "{{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし}}" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. A large zelkova tree on the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Sukagawa City.
== Access == * [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Sukagawa Station]] (approx. 6 minutes by car) * [[Tōhoku Expressway]] [[Sukagawa Interchange|Sukagawa IC]] (approx. 10 minutes by car)

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Nikaidō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = [[Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Sukagawa City]] 78 Miyasaki-cho |coordinates = {{Coord|37|17|22.78|N|140|22|28.06|E|region:JP-07_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nikaidō Shrine}} |Deities = [[Nikaidō clan]] |Rank = |consecration_year = |Main hall = |Alias = |Special designation = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = }}
'''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[Sukagawa City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. It enshrines the [[Nikaidō clan]], the lords of the castle.
The "{{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし}}" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. A large zelkova tree on the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Sukagawa City.
== Access == * [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Sukagawa Station]] (approx. 6 minutes by car) * [[Tōhoku Expressway]] [[Sukagawa Interchange|Sukagawa IC]] (approx. 10 minutes by car)

'''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[Sukagawa City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. It enshrines the [[Nikaidō clan]], the lords of the castle.
The "{{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし}}" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. A large zelkova tree on the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Sukagawa City.
== Access == * [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Sukagawa Station]] (approx. 6 minutes by car) * [[Tōhoku Expressway]] [[Sukagawa Interchange|Sukagawa IC]] (approx. 10 minutes by car)

{{Shrine |name = Nikaidō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = [[Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Sukagawa City]] 78 Miyasaki-cho |coordinates = {{Coord|37|17|22.78|N|140|22|28.06|E|region:JP-07_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nikaidō Shrine}} |Deities = [[Nikaidō clan]] |Rank = |consecration_year = |Main hall = |Alias = |Special designation = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = }}
'''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[Sukagawa City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. It enshrines the [[Nikaidō clan]], the lords of the castle.
The "{{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし}}" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. A large zelkova tree on the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Sukagawa City.
== Access == * [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Sukagawa Station]] (approx. 6 minutes by car) * [[Tōhoku Expressway]] [[Sukagawa Interchange|Sukagawa IC]] (approx. 10 minutes by car)
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * [[Sukagawa Castle]] * {{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine] Fukushima Prefecture Tourism Information Site * [https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine] Sukagawa City Tourism and Product Promotion Association

'''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[Sukagawa City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. It enshrines the [[Nikaidō clan]], the lords of the castle.
The "{{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし}}" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. '''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[Sukagawa City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. It enshrines the [[Nikaidō clan]], the lords of the castle.
The "{{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし}}" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. A large zelkova tree on the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Sukagawa City.

{{Shrine |name = Nikaidō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = [[Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Sukagawa City]] 78 Miyasaki-cho |coordinates = {{Coord|37|17|22.78|N|140|22|28.06|E|region:JP-07_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nikaidō Shrine}} |Deities = [[Nikaidō clan]] |Rank = |consecration_year = |Main hall = |Alias = |Special designation = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = }}
'''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[Sukagawa City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. It enshrines the [[Nikaidō clan]], the lords of the castle.
The "{{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし}}" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. A large zelkova tree on the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Sukagawa City.
== Access == * [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Sukagawa Station]] (approx. 6 minutes by car) * [[Tōhoku Expressway]] [[Sukagawa Interchange|Sukagawa IC]] (approx. 10 minutes by car)
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * [[Sukagawa Castle]] * {{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine] Fukushima Prefecture Tourism Information Site * [https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine] Sukagawa City Tourism and Product Promotion Association

'''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[Sukagawa City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. It enshrines the [[Nikaidō clan]], the lords of the castle.
The "{{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし}}" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. A large zelkova tree on the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Sukagawa City.
== Access == * [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Sukagawa Station]] (approx. 6 minutes by car) * [[Tōhoku Expressway]] [[Sukagawa Interchange|Sukagawa IC]] (approx. 10 minutes by car)

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Nikaidō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = [[Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Sukagawa City]] 78 Miyasaki-cho |coordinates = {{Coord|37|17|22.78|N|140|22|28.06|E|region:JP-07_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nikaidō Shrine}} |Deities = [[Nikaidō clan]] |Rank = |consecration_year = |Main hall = |Alias = |Special designation = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = }}
'''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[Sukagawa City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. It enshrines the [[Nikaidō clan]], the lords of the castle.
The "{{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし|12=draft}}" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. A large zelkova tree on the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Sukagawa City.
== Access == * [[East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[Tōhoku Main Line]] [[Sukagawa Station]] (approx. 6 minutes by car) * [[Tōhoku Expressway]] [[Sukagawa Interchange|Sukagawa IC]] (approx. 10 minutes by car)
== Notes == <references/> == See Also == * [[Sukagawa Castle]] * {{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 二階堂神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[福島県]][[須賀川市]]宮先町78 |位置 = {{Coord|37|17|22.78|N|140|22|28.06|E|region:JP-07_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=二階堂神社}} |祭神 = [[二階堂氏]] |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = }}
'''二階堂神社'''（にかいどうじんじゃ）は、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]の[[須賀川城]]址に鎮座する[[神社]]。城主の[[二階堂氏]]を祀る。
「日本三大火祭り」と称される毎年11月の「[[松明あかし]]」の御神火はこの神社で採火され、須賀川市内を巡り、会場の翠ヶ丘公園へ運ばれる<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 二階堂神社]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html 二階堂神社]</ref>。境内の大ケヤキは須賀川市天然記念物に指定されている。
== 交通 == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]] [[東北本線]] [[須賀川駅]]（車で約6分） *[[東北自動車道]][[須賀川インターチェンジ|須賀川IC]]（車で約10分）
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 関連項目 == * [[須賀川城]] * [[松明あかし]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 二階堂神社] 福島県観光情報サイト * [https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html 二階堂神社] 須賀川市観光物産振興協会
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=福島県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にかいとうしんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:須賀川市の歴史]] [[Category:須賀川市の建築物]] [[Category:二階堂氏|しんしや]]

'''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[:en:Sukagawa City|Sukagawa City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It enshrines the [[:en:Nikaidō clan|Nikaidō clan]], the lords of the castle.
The "{{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu Akashi|qq=}}" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. A large zelkova tree on the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Sukagawa City.
== Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] [[:en:Sukagawa Station|Sukagawa Station]] (approx. 6 minutes by car) * [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]] [[Sukagawa Interchange|Sukagawa IC]] (approx. 10 minutes by car)
== Notes == <references/> == See Also == * [[Sukagawa Castle]] * {{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu Akashi|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine] Fukushima Prefecture Tourism Information Site * [https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine] Sukagawa City Tourism and Product Promotion Association

'''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[:en:Sukagawa City|Sukagawa City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It enshrines the [[:en:Nikaidō clan|Nikaidō clan]], the lords of the castle.
The "{{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu Akashi|qq=}}" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. A large zelkova tree on the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Sukagawa City.
== Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] [[:en:Sukagawa Station|Sukagawa Station]] (approx. 6 minutes by car) * [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]] [[Sukagawa Interchange|Sukagawa IC]] (approx. 10 minutes by car)
== Notes == <references/> == See Also == * [[Sukagawa Castle]] * {{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu Akashi|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine] Fukushima Prefecture Tourism Information Site * [https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine] Sukagawa City Tourism and Product Promotion Association

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二階堂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二階堂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二階堂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q123115141]] Wikidata name: Nikaidō Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nikaidō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} [[:en:Sukagawa City|Sukagawa City]] 78 Miyasaki-cho |coordinates = {{Coord|37.2896611|N|140.3744611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Nikaidō clan|en|Nikaidō clan|WD=Q2000952|lt=Nikaidō clan}} |Rank = |consecration_year = |Main hall = |Alias = |Special designation = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = |prefecture= {{ill|Sukagawa|WD=Q819664}} }}
'''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[:en:Sukagawa City|Sukagawa City]], {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It enshrines the {{ill|Nikaidō clan|en|Nikaidō clan|WD=Q2000952|lt=Nikaidō clan}}, the lords of the castle.
The "{{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu Akashi|qq=}}" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. A large zelkova tree on the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Sukagawa City.
== Access == * {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}} {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|en|Tōhoku Main Line|WD=Q681190|lt=Tōhoku Main Line}} {{ill|Sukagawa Station|en|Sukagawa Station|WD=Q876991|lt=Sukagawa Station}} (approx. 6 minutes by car) * {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tōhoku Expressway}} [[Sukagawa Interchange|Sukagawa IC]] (approx. 10 minutes by car)
== Notes == <references/> == See Also == * [[Sukagawa Castle]] * {{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu Akashi|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine] Fukushima Prefecture Tourism Information Site * [https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine] Sukagawa City Tourism and Product Promotion Association

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二階堂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二階堂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二階堂神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nikaidō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}} [[:en:Sukagawa City|Sukagawa City]] 78 Miyasaki-cho |coordinates = {{Coord|37.2896611|N|140.3744611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Nikaidō clan|en|Nikaidō clan|WD=Q2000952|lt=Nikaidō clan|1=Nikaidō clan}} |Rank = |consecration_year = |Main hall = |Alias = |Special designation = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = |prefecture= {{ill|Sukagawa|WD=Q819664|1=Sukagawa, Fukushima}} }}
'''Nikaidō Shrine''' (にかいどうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located on the site of [[Sukagawa Castle]] in [[:en:Sukagawa City|Sukagawa City]], {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It enshrines the {{ill|Nikaidō clan|en|Nikaidō clan|WD=Q2000952|lt=Nikaidō clan|1=Nikaidō clan}}, the lords of the castle.
The "{{ill|Taimatsu Akashi|ja|松明あかし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu Akashi|qq=}}" fire festival, which is considered one of "Japan’s Three Great Fire Festivals," takes the sacred fire from this shrine every November. The fire is carried throughout Sukagawa City and brought to the venue at Midorigaoka Park<ref>[https://www.tif.ne.jp/jp/spot.html?spot=1736 Nikaidō Shrine]</ref><ref>[https://www.sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/learn/page04.html Nikaidō Shrine]</ref>. A large zelkova tree on the shrine grounds is designated as a natural monument of Sukagawa City.
